citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
The IACUC has three members (Chair, AV, and Public Member). The Public Member has been considered to be the non-
affiliated member by the institution, however the Public Member's job is at a nearby business that was described in the
inspection as also a customer at times to this facility. Having a business (customer/client) relationship with this facility is
an affiliation and the Public Member cannot be considered the non-affiliated member. The IACUC must have a member
that is not affiliated in any way with the facility other than as a member of the IACUC, and shall not be a member of the
immediate family of a person who is affiliated with the facility. Ensure that the IACUC has a member appointed by the
CEO that is not affiliated with the facility and represents the general community interests in the proper care and treatment
of animals.
***Correct by August 31, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.31(b)(3)(ii) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
The IACUC has three members (Chair, AV, and Public Member). The Public Member has been considered to be the non-
affiliated member by the institution, however the Public Member's job is at a nearby business that was described in the 
inspection as also a customer at times to this facility. Having a business (customer/client) relationship with this facility is 
an affiliation and the Public Member cannot be considered the non-affiliated member. The IACUC must have a member 
that is not affiliated in any way with the facility other than as a member of the IACUC, and shall not be a member of the 
immediate family of a person who is affiliated with the facility. Ensure that the IACUC has a member appointed by the 
CEO that is not affiliated with the facility and represents the general community interests in the proper care and treatment 
of animals.  
***Correct by August 31, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
